league.name: Eredivisie
league.description: The Netherlands' top league is a classic football learning lab: Ajax, PSV, and Feyenoord drive the title story, while smaller clubs often develop future European stars.
league.styleOfPlay: Attack-minded and academy-driven, with a strong emphasis on technical midfielders, wingers, possession ideas, and flexible Dutch positional concepts.
famousClubs: Ajax — Amsterdam academy and European history
PSV Eindhoven — title regular with attacking identity
Feyenoord — Rotterdam power and De Kuip atmosphere
AZ Alkmaar — smart recruitment and youth development
FC Twente — strong regional support in Enschede
famousPlayers: Johan Cruyff — Ajax legend and Dutch football icon
Ruud Gullit — Feyenoord and PSV graduate before Milan fame
Ruud van Nistelrooy — PSV striker before Manchester United
league.historySummary: Dutch clubs shaped modern football through Total Football, academy coaching, and European success. Ajax, PSV, and Feyenoord remain the big three, but the league's broader value is how quickly young players can become important.
rivalries: PSV vs Ajax — title-race heavyweight matchup
Twente vs Heracles — eastern Netherlands derby
league.fanGuide: Learn the big three first, especially De Klassieker. Watch how Dutch teams build attacks and trust young players; it is one of the best leagues for learning football patterns.

teams.name: Ajax
teams.shortHistory: Ajax are the academy and ideas club: Total Football, European Cups, and a conveyor belt of players who shape bigger leagues.
teams.fanGuide: White shirt with a red central stripe; Amsterdam confidence. De Klassieker against Feyenoord is essential.
legends: Johan Cruyff — club and football philosophy icon

teams.name: PSV Eindhoven
teams.shortHistory: PSV began as the Philips works team and became one of Dutch football's title powers, known for attacking teams and export-ready forwards.
teams.fanGuide: Red and white stripes; Eindhoven identity. PSV vs Ajax often feels like a title checkpoint.
legends: Arjen Robben — winger before European fame

teams.name: Feyenoord
teams.shortHistory: Feyenoord are Rotterdam's working-class giant, with European history and one of the Netherlands' most intense stadium atmospheres at De Kuip.
teams.fanGuide: Red and white halves; Rotterdam edge. De Klassieker vs Ajax is the first rivalry to learn.

teams.name: AZ Alkmaar
teams.shortHistory: AZ are a smart-recruitment club with title history, strong analytics reputation, and a productive youth pathway outside the Dutch big three.
teams.fanGuide: Red and white from Alkmaar; watch for clever recruitment and academy players taking senior responsibility.

teams.name: FC Twente
teams.shortHistory: Twente carry strong regional support in Enschede and won the Dutch title in 2010, a rare break in the big-three pattern.
teams.fanGuide: Red shirts; De Grolsch Veste is loud when Twente are pushing near the top. The Heracles derby is the local one.

players.name: Anton Gaaei
players.quickFact: Denmark right-back whose attacking runs and crossing emerged at Ajax after breaking through in Danish football.
players.playingStyle: Attacking right-back who pushes high, whips in crosses, and recovers quickly to defend one-on-ones.
quizHints: Denmark international full-back who plays on the right side.
Young overlapping defender known for pace and delivery from wide areas.
Full-back whose first name is Anton and surname is Gaaei.

players.name: Davy Klaassen
players.quickFact: Ajax academy midfielder known for timing late runs into the box and returning to Amsterdam across his career.
players.playingStyle: Intelligent midfielder who reads second balls, arrives late to score, and keeps combinations simple.
quizHints: Came through Ajax before spells in England and Germany.
Dutch midfielder known for smart penalty-area arrivals.
Central midfielder listed with Ajax in FootyBrain.

players.name: Oleksandr Zinchenko
players.quickFact: Ukraine left-back who won the Premier League with Manchester City before Ajax and remains a technical outlet for the national team.
players.playingStyle: Inverted left-back who combines in midfield, crosses early, and defends aggressively in duels.
quizHints: Ukraine left-back in yellow and blue who played for Manchester City.
Ajax full-back — not Artem Dovbyk the striker.
Oleksandr who tucks inside from left-back to help build play.

players.name: Steven Berghuis
players.quickFact: Left-footed Netherlands attacker with major Eredivisie spells at Feyenoord and Ajax.
players.playingStyle: Technical left-footer who cuts inside to combine, shoot, or deliver final passes from the right half-space.
quizHints: Dutch attacker who crossed the Ajax-Feyenoord divide.
Left-footed creator often used from the right or as a midfielder.
Listed with Ajax in this FootyBrain dataset.

players.name: Wout Weghorst
players.quickFact: Netherlands striker remembered for two dramatic goals against Argentina at the 2022 World Cup.
players.playingStyle: Tall target striker who presses hard, contests aerial balls, and finishes from crosses and loose chances.
quizHints: Tall Dutch striker who has played in Germany, England, and the Netherlands.
Scored a late free-kick routine goal against Argentina in 2022.
Centre-forward listed with Ajax in FootyBrain.

players.name: Youri Regeer
players.quickFact: Netherlands midfielder who rose through Ajax’s academy as a versatile central player with passing range and tactical discipline.
players.playingStyle: Intelligent midfielder who recycles possession, presses intelligently, and supports both defensive screens and attacking phases.
quizHints: Netherlands youth international midfielder from Ajax’s academy system.
Central player known for positioning, passing, and calm control in midfield.
Midfielder whose first name is Youri and surname is Regeer.

players.name: Alexandre Penetra
players.quickFact: Portugal centre-back who became an AZ Alkmaar defensive regular with composure on the ball and strong aerial defending.
players.playingStyle: Calm centre-back who defends the box with authority, wins headers, and builds play with tidy passing.
quizHints: Portugal youth international centre-back at AZ Alkmaar.
Left-footed defender comfortable stepping out from the back line.
Centre-back whose first name is Alexandre and surname is Penetra.

players.name: Jizz Hornkamp
players.quickFact: Netherlands striker whose goals in the Eredivisie with Heerenveen and AZ made him a physical forward threat in the box.
players.playingStyle: Target forward who links play, attacks crosses, and finishes with power and timing near goal.
quizHints: Netherlands centre-forward who scored regularly in the Eredivisie.
Tall striker known for hold-up play and finishing inside the area.
Forward whose first name is Jizz and surname is Hornkamp.

players.name: Jordy Clasie
players.quickFact: Netherlands midfielder who won the Eredivisie with Feyenoord and later returned to AZ as a calm possession recycler.
players.playingStyle: Disciplined holding midfielder who keeps rhythm with short passes, tactical fouls when needed, and steady positioning.
quizHints: Part of the Netherlands squad that reached the 2014 World Cup semi-finals.
Played for Southampton in the Premier League before returning to Dutch football.
AZ Alkmaar midfielder whose first name is Jordy.

players.name: Peer Koopmeiners
players.quickFact: Netherlands midfielder in the Koopmeiners family who followed an AZ Alkmaar academy path into senior Eredivisie minutes.
players.playingStyle: Technical midfielder who circulates possession, presses intelligently, and links defence to attack with tidy passing.
quizHints: Netherlands midfielder listed with AZ Alkmaar in FootyBrain.
Younger Koopmeiners name in Dutch football — related to Teun Koopmeiners.
AZ Alkmaar midfielder whose first name is Peer and surname is Koopmeiners.

players.name: Wouter Goes
players.quickFact: Netherlands centre-back who broke through young at AZ Alkmaar with composure, aerial defending, and modern ball-playing traits.
players.playingStyle: Calm centre-back who steps out to intercept, wins headers, and builds play with tidy passing under pressure.
quizHints: Netherlands youth international centre-back at AZ Alkmaar.

Centre-back whose first name is Wouter and surname is Goes.

Box-to-box player known for late runs and pressing intensity.
Midfielder whose first name is Arno and surname is Verschueren.
